facts.text: Cavanagh Communities Corporation bought the land in 1969 and named the new development Rotonda West.
The area was once a cattle ranch owned by William and Alfred Vanderbilt, direct descendants of Cornelius Vanderbilt, before being sold in 1969.
Rotonda West’s layout copies a World War II airfield, with four 3,000‑ft runways arranged like a wagon wheel and a circular taxiway around the edge.
At the heart of the community sits the Rotonda Community Park and Preserve, a 2.4‑km‑circumference hub that hosts a playground, tennis courts, and a walking path.
Rotonda West is part of the Punta Gorda Metropolitan Statistical Area, which falls within the larger North Port‑Bradenton Combined Statistical Area.
A second project, Rotonda East, was abandoned when the Army Corps of Engineers denied a permit to drain the wetlands, preserving the natural landscape.
